dhp209:1: Having applied himself to what was not his own task,
dhp209:2: and not having applied himself to what was,
dhp209:3: having disregarded the goal to grasp at what he held dear,
dhp209:4: he now envies those who kept after themselves, took themselves to task.
dhp210:1: Don’t ever–regardless– be conjoined with what’s dear
dhp210:3: It’s painful not to see what’s dear
dhp211:2: for it’s dreadful to be far from what’s dear.
dhp211:3: No bonds are found for those for whom
dhp212:1: From what’s dear is born grief,
dhp212:2: from what’s dear is born fear.
dhp212:3: For one freed from what’s dear
dhp213:1: From what’s loved is born grief,
dhp213:2: from what’s loved is born fear.
dhp213:3: For one freed from what’s loved
dhp218:1: If you’ve given birth to a wish for what can’t be expressed,
dhp218:3: your mind not enmeshed in sensual passions:
dhp218:4: you’re said to be in the up-flowing stream.
dhp219:3: His kin, his friends, his companions,
dhp220:2: when you’ve done good & gone from this world to the world beyond,
dhp220:4: as kin, someone dear come home.
